message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step approach:
1) Identified each main REG node and its explicit cross-references:
- REG-032 (Art17(1)) references Art 6(1)(a) and Art 9(2)(a) for consent, and Art 21(1) and 21(2) for objections. Cross-references provided: REG-001, REG-006, REG-039, REG-040.
- REG-021 (Art13(3)) requires inclusion of Art 13(2) information when further processing is intended. Cross-reference provided: REG-020.
- REG-026 (Art14(4)) requires inclusion of Art 14(2) information when further processing is intended (in the Art 14 context). Cross-reference provided: REG-024.

2) Analyzed the policy chunk:
- States data are collected on the basis of consent for providing services; not used for any other purposes or shared with third parties.
- States data are removed upon withdrawal of consent or request to terminate services.
- Mentions comments and cookies.

3) REG-032 analysis (Art17(1)):
- GDPR condition (b) requires erasure upon consent withdrawal only “where there is no other legal ground for the processing.” The policy promises erasure upon withdrawal of consent without this caveat, which is stricter than GDPR requires—an over-compliance/severity deviation.
- Other Art17(1) grounds and references (Art 21(1), 21(2)) are not contradicted; omissions are treated as Non-Deviation per instructions.

4) REG-021 analysis (Art13(3)):
- GDPR allows further processing provided data subjects are informed beforehand with Art 13(2) details. The policy explicitly disallows any further purposes. This is stricter than GDPR permits; classify as a severity deviation. Not an omission, because the policy affirmatively bans further purposes.

5) REG-026 analysis (Art14(4)):
- Parallels Art 13(3) but in the Art 14 (indirect collection) context: GDPR allows further processing with prior information including Art 14(2) items. The policy categorically disallows any other purposes, again stricter than GDPR; classify as a severity deviation. Although the policy describes direct collection, the explicit ban on further purposes is a policy constraint, not an omission.

6) Ensured each deviation has exact GDPR and policy quotes and a concise mismatch statement and justification.
</deliberation>
```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-032",
    "clause": "Art17(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-001", "REG-006", "REG-039", "REG-040"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "the data subject withdraws consent on which the processing is based according to point (a) of Article 6(1), or point (a) of Article 9(2), and where there is no other legal ground for the processing",
        "policy_quote": "It is removed upon your withdrawal of consent or your request to terminate these services.",
        "mismatch": "The policy commits to erase data upon consent withdrawal regardless of whether another legal ground exists, while GDPR requires erasure only if no other legal ground remains.",
        "analysis": "GDPR allows continued processing on another lawful basis after consent withdrawal; the policy’s unconditional erasure is stricter than required."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-021",
    "clause": "Art13(3)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-020"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "Where the controller intends to further process the personal data for a purpose other than that for which the personal data were collected, the controller shall provide the data subject prior to that further processing with information on that other purpose and with any relevant further information as referred to in paragraph 2.",
        "policy_quote": "Your data is not used for any other purposes or shared with third parties.",
        "mismatch": "GDPR permits further processing with prior information, whereas the policy categorically forbids any other purposes.",
        "analysis": "By banning further purposes entirely, the policy imposes a stricter constraint than GDPR, which would allow such processing with advance notice including Art 13(2) details."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-026",
    "clause": "Art14(4)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-024"],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Severity deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "Where the controller intends to further process the personal data for a purpose other than that for which the personal data were obtained, the controller shall provide the data subject prior to that further processing with information on that other purpose and with any relevant further information as referred to in paragraph 2.",
        "policy_quote": "Your data is not used for any other purposes or shared with third parties.",
        "mismatch": "GDPR allows further processing with prior information (including Art 14(2) items), while the policy categorically prohibits any other purposes.",
        "analysis": "The policy’s explicit prohibition on further purposes is stricter than GDPR’s conditional allowance to further process with prior disclosure; this is an over-compliance."
      }
    ]
  }
]
```
